28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass
175

178	def apply_lora_mlp_swiglu(self, X, inplace = True):
179	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.gate_proj)
180	    gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS = get_lora_parameters(self.gate_proj)
181	    upW,     upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS = get_lora_parameters(self.  up_proj)
182	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)
183	    out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X,
184	                         gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
185	                         upW,     upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
186	                         downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
187	                         swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel,
188	                         inplace,)
189	    return out
190	pass

198	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,

366	def apply_lora_qkv(self, X, inplace = True):
367	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.q_proj)
368	    QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS = get_lora_parameters(self.q_proj)
369	    KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS = get_lora_parameters(self.k_proj)
370	    VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS = get_lora_parameters(self.v_proj)
371	    Q, K, V = LoRA_QKV.apply(X,
372	        QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
373	        KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
374	        VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
375	        inplace,
376	    )
377	    return Q, K, V

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass
458

460	def apply_lora_o(self, X):
461	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.o_proj)
462	    OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS = get_lora_parameters(self.o_proj)
463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O
465	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass

65	def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
66	    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
68	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize

292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass

299	        device = W.device

1618	def create_ollama_modelfile(tokenizer, gguf_location):
1619	    """
1620	        Creates an Ollama Modelfile.
1621	        Use ollama.create(model = "new_ollama_model", modelfile = modelfile)
1622	    """
1623	    modelfile = getattr(tokenizer, "_ollama_modelfile", None)
1624	    if modelfile is None: return None
1625	
1626	    FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER = "⚫@✅#🦥__FILE_LOCATION__⚡@🦥#⛵"
1627	    EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER     = "⚫@✅#🦥__EOS_TOKEN__⚡@🦥#⛵"
1628	    LEFT_BRACKET_REPLACER  = "⚫@✅#🦥"
1629	    RIGHT_BRACKET_REPLACER = "⚡@🦥#⛵"
1630	
1631	    # Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1087
1632	    # We must convert all {'s and }'s but keep {__FILE_LOCATION__} intact
1633	    modelfile = modelfile\
1634	        .replace("{__FILE_LOCATION__}", FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER)\
1635	        .replace("{__EOS_TOKEN__}",     EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER)\
1636	        .replace("{", LEFT_BRACKET_REPLACER)\
1637	        .replace("}", RIGHT_BRACKET_REPLACER)
1638	
1639	    # Revert {__FILE_LOCATION__} back
1640	    modelfile = modelfile\
1641	        .replace(FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER, "{__FILE_LOCATION__}")\
1642	        .replace(EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER,     "{__EOS_TOKEN__}")
1643	
1644	    if "__EOS_TOKEN__" in modelfile:
1645	        modelfile = modelfile.format(
1646	            __FILE_LOCATION__  = gguf_location,
1647	            __EOS_TOKEN__      = tokenizer.eos_token,
1648	        )
1649	    else:
1650	        modelfile = modelfile.format(
1651	            __FILE_LOCATION__  = gguf_location,
1652	        )
1653	    pass
1654	
1655	    modelfile = modelfile\
1656	        .replace("⚫@✅#🦥", "{")\
1657	        .replace("⚡@🦥#⛵", "}")\
1658	        .rstrip()
1659	
1660	    return modelfile
1661	pass

1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """
1819	    if tokenizer is None:
1820	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

1849	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)

1855	            makefile = None
1856	        except:
1857	            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp
1858	            if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:

1860	                python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1861	                python_install.wait()

205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()
301	        pass
302	        print("Done.")
303	    pass
304

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())

128	        elif hasattr(past_key_values, "get_seq_length") and past_key_values.get_seq_length() == 0:
129	            past_key_values = None

132	            bs, cache_length = input_ids.shape

135	            # Get to the base model

140	            if hasattr(base_model, "_prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position"):

144	                        return "device" in sig.parameters

153	                    "cache_position": torch.arange(cache_length, cache_length+1, device=input_ids.device),
154	                    "batch_size": bs,
155	                    "config": self.config,

157	                }

192	def LlamaAttention_fast_forward_inference(
193	    self,
194	    hidden_states:  torch.Tensor,
195	    past_key_value: Optional[Tuple[torch.Tensor]],
196	    position_ids,
197	    do_prefill = False,
198	    attention_mask = None,
199	):
200	    """
201	        https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/main/src/transformers/models/llama/modeling_llama.py#L406
202	        Fast inference using KV cache.
203	        QK^T can be computed in 4 chunks
204	
205	        [Q, q] @ [K, k].T where q, k are the new tokens.
206	        [QK^T, Qk^T]
207	        [qK^T, qk^T]
208	
209	        Since the attention mask wipes Qk^T, we just get
210	        [QK^T,    0]
211	        [qK^T, qk^T]
212	
213	        Since softmax is row-wise, we get
214	        softmax([QK^T,    0])
215	        softmax([qK^T, qk^T])
216	
217	        We then multiply by   [V]
218	                              [v]
219	        softmax([QK^T,    0]) [softmax(QK^T)V] *
220	        softmax([qK^T, qk^T]) [softmax([qK^T, qk^T]) @ [V, v]]
221	
222	        But notice * [softmax(QK^T)V] is just the last attention.
223	        We just need to compute the last final row.
224	
225	        This means we can pass in a row of Q, but we need to
226	        remember K and V, which are called the KV cache.
227	    """
228	    Xn = hidden_states
229	    bsz, _, hd = hidden_states.size()
230	    K1, V1 = past_key_value
231	    dtype = Xn.dtype
232	
233	    n_heads    = self.config.num_attention_heads
234	    n_groups   = self.num_key_value_groups
235	    n_kv_heads = self.config.num_key_value_heads
236	    head_dim   = self.head_dim
237	    # assert(n_kv_heads * n_groups == n_heads)
238	
239	    hidden_size = self.config.hidden_size
240	    attention_size = n_heads*head_dim
241	    seq_len = K1.shape[-2]
242	    kv_seq_len = seq_len + 1
243	
244	    # Prefill phase
245	    # if not hasattr(self, "paged_attention"):
246	    device = hidden_states.device
247	    if do_prefill:
248	        self.paged_attention = torch.empty((KV_CACHE_INCREMENT+seq_len+1, 2, bsz, n_kv_heads, head_dim), dtype = dtype, device = device)
249	        self.paged_attention_K = self.paged_attention[:,0]
250	        self.paged_attention_V = self.paged_attention[:,1]
251	        self.paged_attention_K[:seq_len] = K1.permute(2, 0, 1, 3)
252	        self.paged_attention_V[:seq_len] = V1.permute(2, 0, 1, 3)
253	        self.temp_QA = torch.empty((2, bsz, 1, attention_size), dtype = dtype, device = device)
254	        self.temp_KV = torch.empty((2, bsz, 1, n_kv_heads*head_dim), dtype = dtype, device = device)
255	        self.RH_Q = torch.empty((bsz, n_heads, 1, head_dim), dtype = dtype, device = device)
256	
257	        # Mistral Nemo 12b has weird dimensions
258	        if attention_size != hidden_size:
259	            self.temp_O = torch.empty((1, bsz, hidden_size), dtype = dtype, device = device)
260	        else:
261	            self.temp_O = self.temp_QA[1][:,:,:hidden_size]
262	        pass
263	
264	        self.attention = torch.empty((bsz, n_heads, 1, KV_CACHE_INCREMENT+seq_len), dtype = dtype, device = device)
265	        self.scalar = 1.0 / math_sqrt(self.head_dim)
266	        self.half_head_dim = head_dim // 2
267	    elif kv_seq_len >= self.paged_attention.shape[0]:
268	        self.paged_attention.resize_((self.paged_attention.shape[0]+KV_CACHE_INCREMENT, 2, bsz, n_kv_heads, head_dim))
269	        self.paged_attention_K = self.paged_attention[:,0]
270	        self.paged_attention_V = self.paged_attention[:,1]
271	        self.attention.resize_((bsz, n_heads, 1, self.attention.shape[-1]+KV_CACHE_INCREMENT))
272	    pass
273	
274	    Qn = fast_linear_forward(self.q_proj, Xn, out = self.temp_QA[0])
275	    Kn = fast_linear_forward(self.k_proj, Xn, out = self.temp_KV[0])
276	    Vn = fast_linear_forward(self.v_proj, Xn, out = self.temp_KV[1])
277	    Qn = Qn.view(bsz, 1, n_heads,    head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
278	    Kn = Kn.view(bsz, 1, n_kv_heads, head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
279	    Vn = Vn.view(bsz, 1, n_kv_heads, head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
280	
281	    # cos, sin = self.rotary_emb(Vn, seq_len = kv_seq_len)
282	    # Qn, Kn = inplace_rope_embedding(Qn, Kn, cos, sin, position_ids)
283	
284	    # Need to do it prior 2 steps before hitting full on short KV cache
285	    # or else error
286	    self.rotary_emb.extend_rope_embedding(Vn, seq_len + 2)
287	    cos, sin = self.rotary_emb.get_cached(kv_seq_len, Qn.device.index)
288	    cos = cos[position_ids].unsqueeze(1)
289	    sin = sin[position_ids].unsqueeze(1)
290	    h = self.half_head_dim
291	
292	    RH_Q = self.RH_Q
293	    RH_Q[:,:,:,:h] = Qn[:,:,:,h:]
294	    RH_Q[:,:,:,h:] = Qn[:,:,:,:h]
295	    RH_Q[:,:,:,:h].neg_() # torch.neg(RH_Q[:,:,:,:h], out = RH_Q[:,:,:,:h])
296	    Qn *= cos
297	    Qn.addcmul_(RH_Q, sin)
298	
299	    RH_K = RH_Q[:,:n_kv_heads,:,:] # torch.empty((n_kv_heads, 1, head_dim), dtype = dtype, device = "cuda:0")
300	    RH_K[:,:,:,:h] = Kn[:,:,:,h:]
301	    RH_K[:,:,:,h:] = Kn[:,:,:,:h]
302	    RH_K[:,:,:,:h].neg_() #torch.neg(RH_K[:,:,:,:h], out = RH_K[:,:,:,:h])
303	    Kn *= cos
304	    Kn.addcmul_(RH_K, sin)
305	
306	    # New KV cache
307	    # Kn = torch.cat([K1, Kn], dim = 2)
308	    # Vn = torch.cat([V1, Vn], dim = 2)
309	    self.paged_attention_K[seq_len] = Kn.permute(2, 0, 1, 3)
310	    self.paged_attention_V[seq_len] = Vn.permute(2, 0, 1, 3)
311	    Kn = self.paged_attention_K[:kv_seq_len].permute(1, 2, 0, 3)
312	    Vn = self.paged_attention_V[:kv_seq_len].permute(1, 2, 0, 3)
313	
314	    # Handle sliding windows
315	    sliding_window = getattr(self.config, "sliding_window", None)
316	    if sliding_window is not None and kv_seq_len > sliding_window:
317	        # From https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/main/src/transformers/models/mistral/modeling_mistral.py#L193
318	        slicing_tokens = 1 - sliding_window
319	        Knn = Kn[:, :, slicing_tokens:, :]#.contiguous()
320	        Vnn = Vn[:, :, slicing_tokens:, :]#.contiguous()
321	    else:
322	        Knn, Vnn = Kn, Vn
323	    pass
324	
325	    # Grouped query attention
326	    _, _, cached_len, _ = Knn.shape
327	    if bsz == 1 or not SDPA_HAS_GQA and n_groups != 1:
328	        Knn = Knn[:, :, None, :, :].expand(bsz, n_kv_heads, n_groups, cached_len, head_dim)
329	        Vnn = Vnn[:, :, None, :, :].expand(bsz, n_kv_heads, n_groups, cached_len, head_dim)
330	        Knn = Knn.reshape(bsz, n_heads, cached_len, head_dim)
331	        Vnn = Vnn.reshape(bsz, n_heads, cached_len, head_dim)
332	    pass
333	    # else:
334	    #     Knn, Vnn = Knn, Vnn
335	    # pass
336	
337	    # when qlen==vlen and attn_mask is None, we should use causal attention
338	    Q_len = Qn.shape[-2]
339	    K_len = Knn.shape[-2]
340	    if attention_mask is None and Q_len == K_len:
341	        is_causal = True
342	    else:
343	        is_causal = False
344	    # Attention
345	    if bsz == 1:
346	        Qn *= self.scalar # See https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7805#issuecomment-2153349963
347	        # It seems like doing (Q * scalar) @ K is better than (Q @ K) * scalar to stop overflows
348	        A = torch_matmul(Qn, Knn.transpose(2, 3), out = self.attention[:,:,:,:cached_len])
349	        # if attention_mask is not None: A += attention_mask # Must add attention_mask for batched

352	    else:

354	            A = scaled_dot_product_attention(Qn, Knn, Vnn, attn_mask = attention_mask, is_causal = is_causal, enable_gqa = True)
355	        else:
356	            A = scaled_dot_product_attention(Qn, Knn, Vnn, attn_mask = attention_mask, is_causal = is_causal)

358	    A = A.transpose(1, 2)

361	    return A, (Kn, Vn)

394	def fast_rms_layernorm_inference(self, X, XX = None, XX2 = None, variance = None):
395	    old_dtype = X.dtype
396	    if XX is None:
397	        XX = X.to(torch.float32)
398	        variance = XX.square().mean(-1, keepdim = True)
399	    else:
400	        XX.copy_(X)
401	        torch_mean(torch_square(XX, out = XX2), -1, keepdim = True, out = variance)
402	    pass
403	    variance += self.variance_epsilon
404	    XX *= variance.rsqrt_()
405	
406	    if XX is None: X = XX.to(old_dtype)
407	    else: X.copy_(XX)
408	
409	    X *= self.weight
410	    return X

414	def fast_rms_layernorm_inference_gemma(self, X, out_weight = None):
415	    XX = X.to(torch.float32)
416	    variance = XX.square().mean(-1, keepdim = True)
417	    variance += self.variance_epsilon
418	    XX *= variance.rsqrt_()
419	
420	    if out_weight is None:
421	        out_weight = self.weight + 1.0
422	    else:
423	        out_weight[:] = self.weight
424	        out_weight += 1.0
425	    pass
426	
427	    XX *= out_weight
428	    return XX.to(X.dtype)

432	# Normal layernorm with mean removal
433	@torch.compile(fullgraph = False, dynamic = True, options = torch_compile_options)
434	def fast_layernorm_compiled(layernorm, X):
435	    old_dtype = X.dtype
436	    X = X.float()
437	    mean = X.mean(-1, keepdim = True)
438	    Xbar = X - mean
439	    X = Xbar * torch.rsqrt(Xbar.square().mean(-1, keepdim = True) + \
440	        layernorm.variance_epsilon) * \
441	        layernorm.weight.float()
442	    return X.to(old_dtype)

447	def LlamaAttention_fast_forward(
448	    self,
449	    hidden_states:       torch.Tensor,

454	    output_attentions:   bool = False,

457	    position_embeddings: Optional[Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]] = None,
458	    *args, **kwargs,
459	) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor], Optional[Tuple[torch.Tensor]]]:
460	
461	    # Clear inference
462	    if hasattr(self, "paged_attention"):
463	        del self.paged_attention_K
464	        del self.paged_attention_V
465	        del self.paged_attention
466	        del self.temp_QA
467	        del self.temp_KV
468	        del self.RH_Q
469	        del self.attention
470	    pass
471	
472	    bsz, q_len, _ = hidden_states.size()
473	
474	    n_heads    = self.config.num_attention_heads
475	    n_groups   = self.num_key_value_groups
476	    n_kv_heads = self.config.num_key_value_heads
477	    head_dim   = self.head_dim
478	    assert(n_kv_heads * n_groups == n_heads)
479	
480	    Q, K, V = self.apply_qkv(self, hidden_states)
481	    Q = Q.view(bsz, q_len, n_heads,    head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
482	    K = K.view(bsz, q_len, n_kv_heads, head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
483	    V = V.view(bsz, q_len, n_kv_heads, head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
484	
485	    kv_seq_len = K.shape[-2]
486	    if past_key_value is not None:
487	        kv_seq_len += past_key_value[0].shape[-2]
488	
489	    if position_embeddings:
490	        cos, sin = position_embeddings
491	    else:
492	        # Extend RoPE dynamically to fit in VRA
493	        rotary_emb = self.rotary_emb
494	        rotary_emb.extend_rope_embedding(V, seq_len = kv_seq_len)
495	
496	        # if position_ids is None:

1787	):
1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",

1819	    if tokenizer is None:

1821	
1822	    arguments = dict(locals())

31	def create_dataset(tokenizer, num_examples: int = None, messages: list[dict] = None):
32	    dataset = create_instruction_dataset(messages)
33	
34	    def _apply_chat_template(example):
35	        chat = tokenizer.apply_chat_template(example["messages"], tokenize=False)
36	        return {"text": chat}
37	
38	    dataset = dataset.map(_apply_chat_template, remove_columns="messages")
39	    if num_examples is not None:
40	        if len(dataset) < num_examples:
41	            num_repeats = num_examples // len(dataset) + 1
42	            dataset = dataset.repeat(num_repeats)
43	        dataset = dataset.select(range(num_examples))
44	
45	    return dataset

48	def describe_param(
49	    param: torch.Tensor,
50	    include_l1: bool = False,
51	    include_l2: bool = False,
52	    include_infinity: bool = False,
53	    as_str: bool = True,
54	) -> dict:
55	    """
56	    Provide a statistical summary of a 2D weight matrix or tensor.
57	    If as_str is True, the summary is returned as a formatted string.
58	    Parameters:
59	        param: torch.Tensor
60	        include_l1 (bool): Whether to include the L1 norm (sum of absolute values).
61	        include_l2 (bool): Whether to include the L2 norm (Frobenius norm).
62	        include_infinity (bool): Whether to include the infinity norm (max absolute value).
63	        as_str (bool): Whether to return the summary as a formatted string.
64	
65	    Returns:
66	        dict: A dictionary with the following statistics:
67	              - shape: Dimensions of the matrix.
68	              - mean: Average value.
69	              - median: Median value.
70	              - std: Standard deviation.
71	              - min: Minimum value.
72	              - max: Maximum value.
73	              - percentile_25: 25th percentile.
74	              - percentile_75: 75th percentile.
75	              Additionally, if enabled:
76	              - L1_norm: Sum of absolute values.
77	              - L2_norm: Euclidean (Frobenius) norm.
78	              - infinity_norm: Maximum absolute value.
79	    """
80	
81	    param = param.float()
82	    summary = {
83	        "shape": param.shape,
84	        "mean": param.mean().cpu().item(),
85	        "std": param.std().cpu().item(),
86	        "min": param.min().cpu().item(),
87	        "max": param.max().cpu().item(),
88	        "percentile_25": param.quantile(0.25).cpu().item(),
89	        "percentile_50": param.quantile(0.5).cpu().item(),
90	        "percentile_75": param.quantile(0.75).cpu().item(),
91	    }
92	
93	    if include_l1:
94	        summary["L1_norm"] = param.abs().sum().cpu().item()
95	    if include_l2:
96	        summary["L2_norm"] = param.norm().cpu().item()
97	    if include_infinity:
98	        summary["infinity_norm"] = param.abs().max().cpu().item()
99	
100	    return format_summary(summary) if as_str else summary
101	
102	
103	def format_summary(stats: dict, precision: int = 6) -> str:
104	    """
105	    Format the statistical summary dictionary for printing.
106	
107	    Parameters:
108	        stats (dict): The dictionary returned by describe_param.
109	        precision (int): Number of decimal places for floating point numbers.
110	
111	    Returns:
112	        str: A formatted string representing the summary.
113	    """
114	    lines = []
115	    for key, value in stats.items():
116	        if isinstance(value, float):
117	            formatted_value = f"{value:.{precision}f}"
118	        elif isinstance(value, (tuple, list)):
119	            # Format each element in tuples or lists (e.g., the shape)
120	            formatted_value = ", ".join(str(v) for v in value)
121	            formatted_value = (
122	                f"({formatted_value})"
123	                if isinstance(value, tuple)
124	                else f"[{formatted_value}]"
125	            )
126	        else:
127	            formatted_value = str(value)
128	        lines.append(f"{key}: {formatted_value}")
129	    return "\n".join(lines)

2503	    self,

2511	
2512	    Args

2514	      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
2515	      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally

2521	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
2522	    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
2523	    del arguments["self"]
2524	    del arguments["torchao_config"]
2525	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
2526	    for _ in range(3):
2527	        gc.collect()
2528	
2529	    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
2530	    from torchao import quantize_
2531	    if torchao_config is None:

2533	        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
2534	    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)
2535

2537	    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:

2539	    else:
2540	        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
2541	    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
2542	        arguments["save_directory"],
2543	        device_map = "auto",
2544	        quantization_config = quantization_config,
2545	        **kwargs,
2546	    )
2547	
2548	    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"
2549	
2550	    if push_to_hub:

2555	    else:

2557	        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)

107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass

215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass

Now let me compile the comprehensive findings:

## Summary: How Unsloth Stores and Accesses LoRA Adapter Weights

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's the complete picture of how LoRA layers are structured and accessed:

### 1. **LoRA Layer Structure (Classes & Attributes)**

LoRA adapters in unsloth use PEFT's implementation. Each LoRA-enabled module contains:

- **`lora_A` dictionary**: Maps adapter names to `nn.Linear` modules containing the A (down-projection) weight matrices
  - Access: `module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` → shape typically `(r, in_features)`
  
- **`lora_B` dictionary**: Maps adapter names to `nn.Linear` modules containing the B (up-projection) weight matrices  
  - Access: `module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight` → shape typically `(out_features, r)`

- **`scaling` dictionary**: Contains the scaling factors per adapter
  - Access: `module.scaling[adapter_name]` → typically `lora_alpha / r`

- **`active_adapters`**: List of currently active adapter names (usually `["default"]` for single adapter)
  - Access: `module.active_adapters` → list of adapter names

- **`lora_dropout` dictionary**: Dropout layers per adapter
  - Access: `module.lora_dropout[active_adapter]`

- **`use_dora` dictionary**: Boolean flag for DoRA (if enabled)
  - Access: `module.use_dora[active_adapter]`

- **`base_layer`**: The underlying frozen base layer
  - Access: `getattr(module, "base_layer", module)` → contains base `.weight` and `.bias`

**Key Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495285025-d5pboc/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-231): `get_lora_parameters()` and `get_lora_parameters_bias()` functions

### 2. **How to Iterate Over LoRA Modules in a Trained Model**

**Pattern 1: Via `named_parameters()` (Simplest for accessing weights)**
```python
def get_peft_weights(model):
    is_lora_weight = lambda name: any(s in name for s in ["lora_A", "lora_B"])
    return {
        name: param for name, param in model.named_parameters() 
        if is_lora_weight(name)
    }

# Usage
for name, param in get_peft_weights(model).items():
    print(name, param.shape)
```
**File Reference:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495285025-d5pboc/tests/utils/data_utils.py` (lines 132-142)

**Pattern 2: Via Direct Module Traversal (For accessing metadata)**
```python
for idx, layer in enumerate(model.model.model.layers):
    # Access individual projections
    q_proj = layer.self_attn.q_proj      # Has lora_A, lora_B, scaling
    k_proj = layer.self_attn.k_proj
    v_proj = layer.self_attn.v_proj
    o_proj = layer.self_attn.o_proj
    
    gate_proj = layer.mlp.gate_proj
    up_proj = layer.mlp.up_proj
    down_proj = layer.mlp.down_proj
    
    # Check if LoRA is attached
    if hasattr(q_proj, "lora_A"):
        adapter_name = q_proj.active_adapters[0]
        lora_A_weight = q_proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
        lora_B_weight = q_proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
        scaling = q_proj.scaling[adapter_name]
```
**File Reference:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495285025-d5pboc/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2822-2895)

**Pattern 3: Via `get_lora_parameters()` helper**
```python
from unsloth.kernels import get_lora_parameters, QUANT_STATE

for proj in [layer.self_attn.q_proj, layer.mlp.gate_proj, ...]:
    W, quant_state, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(proj)
    # W: base weight
    # quant_state: quantization info (if 4-bit)
    # A: lora_A weight matrix
    # B: lora_B weight matrix  
    # scaling: lora_alpha scaling factor
```
**File Reference:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495285025-d5pboc/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-231)

### 3. **Accessing lora_A, lora_B, Rank, Alpha, Target Modules (Configuration)**

**Configuration Access:**
```python
# Get LoRA configuration
if isinstance(model, PeftModelForCausalLM):
    peft_config = model.peft_config["default"]  # Dictionary of configs keyed by adapter name
    
    r = peft_config.r                           # Rank
    lora_alpha = peft_config.lora_alpha         # Alpha scaling
    target_modules = peft_config.target_modules # List of modules to apply LoRA to
    lora_dropout = peft_config.lora_dropout     # Dropout rate
    bias = peft_config.bias                     # Bias type ("none", "all", or "lora_only")
    modules_to_save = peft_config.modules_to_save # Additional modules to fully train
```

**Direct Access to Weights:**
```python
# For a specific layer's projection
q_proj = model.model.model.layers[0].self_attn.q_proj

# Get adapter name
active_adapter = model.active_adapters[0]  # Usually "default"

# Access A and B matrices
lora_A_matrix = q_proj.lora_A[active_adapter].weight  # Shape: (r, in_features)
lora_B_matrix = q_proj.lora_B[active_adapter].weight  # Shape: (out_features, r)
scaling_factor = q_proj.scaling[active_adapter]       # lora_alpha / r
```

**File References:**
- Configuration: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495285025-d5pboc/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2334-2350, 2811-2812, 2772)
- Actual setup: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495285025-d5pboc/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2598-2617)

### 4. **Model Structure (model.model vs model.peft_model)**

**PEFT-Wrapped Model Structure:**
```python
from peft import PeftModelForCausalLM

# After calling FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()
# model is now a PeftModelForCausalLM instance

# Access the underlying base model
base_model = model.model  # The original LlamaForCausalLM (now wrapped)

# Access the actual transformer layers
layers = model.model.model.layers  # List of LlamaDecoderLayer instances

# For each layer, access LoRA modules:
layer = model.model.model.layers[0]
layer.self_attn.q_proj          # Has lora_A, lora_B if in target_modules
layer.self_attn.k_proj
layer.self_attn.v_proj
layer.self_attn.o_proj
layer.mlp.gate_proj             # Has lora_A, lora_B if in target_modules
layer.mlp.up_proj
layer.mlp.down_proj
```

**Key Attributes:**
```python
model.peft_config              # Dict[str, LoraConfig] - Configuration per adapter
model.active_adapters          # List[str] - Currently active adapter names
model.peft_model_type          # String indicating PEFT model type
```

## Summary: How Unsloth Stores and Accesses LoRA Adapter Weights

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's the complete picture of how LoRA layers are structured and accessed:

### 1. **LoRA Layer Structure (Classes & Attributes)**

LoRA adapters in unsloth use PEFT's implementation. Each LoRA-enabled module contains:

- **`lora_A` dictionary**: Maps adapter names to `nn.Linear` modules containing the A (down-projection) weight matrices
  - Access: `module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` → shape typically `(r, in_features)`
  
- **`lora_B` dictionary**: Maps adapter names to `nn.Linear` modules containing the B (up-projection) weight matrices  
  - Access: `module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight` → shape typically `(out_features, r)`

- **`scaling` dictionary**: Contains the scaling factors per adapter
  - Access: `module.scaling[adapter_name]` → typically `lora_alpha / r`

- **`active_adapters`**: List of currently active adapter names (usually `["default"]` for single adapter)
  - Access: `module.active_adapters` → list of adapter names

- **`lora_dropout` dictionary**: Dropout layers per adapter
  - Access: `module.lora_dropout[active_adapter]`

- **`use_dora` dictionary**: Boolean flag for DoRA (if enabled)
  - Access: `module.use_dora[active_adapter]`

- **`base_layer`**: The underlying frozen base layer
  - Access: `getattr(module, "base_layer", module)` → contains base `.weight` and `.bias`

**Key Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495285025-d5pboc/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-231): `get_lora_parameters()` and `get_lora_parameters_bias()` functions

### 2. **How to Iterate Over LoRA Modules in a Trained Model**

**Pattern 1: Via `named_parameters()` (Simplest for accessing weights)**
```python
def get_peft_weights(model):
    is_lora_weight = lambda name: any(s in name for s in ["lora_A", "lora_B"])
    return {
        name: param for name, param in model.named_parameters() 
        if is_lora_weight(name)
    }

# Usage
for name, param in get_peft_weights(model).items():
    print(name, param.shape)
```
**File Reference:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495285025-d5pboc/tests/utils/data_utils.py` (lines 132-142)

**Pattern 2: Via Direct Module Traversal (For accessing metadata)**
```python
for idx, layer in enumerate(model.model.model.layers):
    # Access individual projections
    q_proj = layer.self_attn.q_proj      # Has lora_A, lora_B, scaling
    k_proj = layer.self_attn.k_proj
    v_proj = layer.self_attn.v_proj
    o_proj = layer.self_attn.o_proj
    
    gate_proj = layer.mlp.gate_proj
    up_proj = layer.mlp.up_proj
    down_proj = layer.mlp.down_proj
    
    # Check if LoRA is attached
    if hasattr(q_proj, "lora_A"):
        adapter_name = q_proj.active_adapters[0]
        lora_A_weight = q_proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
        lora_B_weight = q_proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
        scaling = q_proj.scaling[adapter_name]
```

**Pattern 3: Via `get_lora_parameters()` helper**
```python
from unsloth.kernels import get_lora_parameters, QUANT_STATE

for proj in [layer.self_attn.q_proj, layer.mlp.gate_proj, ...]:
    W, quant_state, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(proj)
    # W: base weight
    # quant_state: quantization info (if 4-bit)
    # A: lora_A weight matrix
    # B: lora_B weight matrix  
    # scaling: lora_alpha scaling factor
```
**File Reference:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495285025-d5pboc/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-231)

### 3. **Accessing lora_A, lora_B, Rank, Alpha, Target Modules (Configuration)**

**Configuration Access:**
```python

if isinstance(model, PeftModelForCausalLM):
    peft_config = model.peft_config["default"]  # Dictionary of configs keyed by adapter name
    
    r = peft_config.r                           # Rank
    lora_alpha = peft_config.lora_alpha         # Alpha scaling
    target_modules = peft_config.target_modules # List of modules to apply LoRA to
    lora_dropout = peft_config.lora_dropout     # Dropout rate

    modules_to_save = peft_config.modules_to_save # Additional modules to fully train
```

**Direct Access to Weights:**
```python

# Get adapter name

# Access A and B matrices

lora_B_matrix = q_proj.lora_B[active_adapter].weight  # Shape: (out_features, r)

```

### 4. **Model Structure (model.model vs model.peft_model)**

Perfect! Now let me compile a comprehensive report of my findings:

## Unsloth Library: Save and Export Methods - Comprehensive Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the Unsloth codebase, here's a complete analysis of the save and export methods:

### 1. **Module Structure Overview**

The Unsloth library is organized as follows:
- **Main package**: `/unsloth/`
- **Key files**:
  - `__init__.py` - Main entry point, imports and patches everything
  - `save.py` - Core save/export functionality (2,678 lines)
  - `models/` - Model-specific implementations
  - `trainer.py` - Training-related utilities
  - `chat_templates.py` - Chat template utilities

### 2. **All Existing Save and Export Methods**

#### **Core Standalone Functions** (in `/unsloth/save.py`)

| Function | Line | Signature | Purpose |
|----------|------|-----------|---------|
| `unsloth_save_model` | 205 | `(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method, push_to_hub, ...)` | Main low-level save function. Supports "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit" save methods |
| `save_to_gguf` | 952 | `(model_type, model_dtype, is_sentencepiece, model_directory, quantization_method, ...)` | Converts merged model to GGUF format using llama.cpp. Supports multiple quantization methods (q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q8_0, etc.) |
| `save_to_gguf_generic` | 2276 | `(model, save_directory, tokenizer, quantization_method, quantization_type, repo_id, token)` | Vision/generic model GGUF conversion without llama.cpp integration |
| `patch_saving_functions` | 2569 | `(model, vision=False)` | **Monkey-patches model with save/export methods** |
| `create_huggingface_repo` | 1456 | `(repo_id, exist_ok, token, private)` | Creates a Hugging Face Hub repository |
| `print_quantization_methods` | 109 | `()` | Prints available GGUF quantization options |
| `check_if_sentencepiece_model` | 116 | `(model, temporary_location)` | Checks if model uses SentencePiece tokenizer |
| `create_ollama_modelfile` | 1618 | `(tokenizer, gguf_location)` | Creates Ollama modelfile for GGUF models |
| `push_to_ollama` | 1735 | `(tokenizer, gguf_location, username, model_name, tag)` | Pushes GGUF to Ollama Hub |
| `save_lora_to_custom_dir` | 2129 | `(model, tokenizer, save_directory)` | Saves LoRA adapters to custom directory |

#### **Instance Methods** (Added via Monkey-Patching via `patch_saving_functions`)

These methods are **dynamically added to model objects** using Python's `types.MethodType`:

| Method Name | Source Function | Line | Purpose |
|-------------|-----------------|------|---------|
| `model.push_to_hub_merged` | `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged` | 2456 | Merges LoRA and pushes to HF Hub |
| `model.save_pretrained_merged` | `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged` | 2413 | Merges LoRA and saves locally |
| `model.push_to_hub_gguf` | `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf` | 1951 | Converts to GGUF and pushes to HF Hub |
| `model.save_pretrained_gguf` | `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf` | 1768 | Converts to GGUF and saves locally |
| `model.save_pretrained_torchao` | `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` | 2502 | Quantizes with TorchAO and saves |
| `model.push_to_hub_ggml` | `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub` | 2143 | Converts LoRA to GGML and pushes to HF Hub |
| `model.save_pretrained_ggml` | `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally` | 2211 | Converts LoRA to GGML and saves locally |
| `model.push_to_hub` | `unsloth_push_to_hub` (dynamically generated) | 2586 | Wraps HF's push_to_hub with Unsloth enhancements |

### 3. **How Methods are Exposed/Registered**

#### **Monkey-Patching Mechanism** (in `patch_saving_functions`, lines 2569-2678)

The `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)` function is the central registration point:

1. **Original `push_to_hub` wrapping** (lines 2575-2639):
   - Generates a wrapper function `unsloth_push_to_hub` dynamically using `exec()`
   - Saves original as `model.original_push_to_hub`
   - Adds "unsloth" tag and enhances commit messages
   - Recursively wraps nested models

2. **Method registration** (lines 2657-2676):
   ```python
   # For language models (not vision):
   model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
   model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
   model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
   model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
   model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
   model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
   model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
   
   # For vision models: limited options
   model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
   model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
   model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
   model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
   ```

#### **Where `patch_saving_functions` is Called**

| File | Line | Context |
|------|------|---------|
| `models/llama.py` | 2188 | After loading tokenizer |
| `models/llama.py` | 2204 | After loading model |
| `models/llama.py` | 2902 | Alternative path |
| `models/vision.py` | 702, 703, 918, 964 | For vision models |
| `chat_templates.py` | 2309 | For tokenizer enhancement |

#### **Module Exports** (in `unsloth/__init__.py`, lines 261-266)

```python
from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *              # Exports: __all__ from save.py
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *
```

The `__all__` in `save.py` (lines 48-54) explicitly exports:
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

### 4. **Save Method Types and Capabilities**

#### **A. Merged Models** (`save_pretrained_merged`, `push_to_hub_merged`)
- **Parameters**: `save_method` = "lora" | "merged_16bit" | "merged_4bit"
- **Features**:
  - "lora": Saves only LoRA adapters without merging
  - "merged_16bit": Merges LoRA into float16 (best for GGUF/llama.cpp)
  - "merged_4bit": Merges into int4 (for DPO/HF inference)
  - Defined in: `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged` (line 1321), `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged` (line 1364)

#### **B. GGUF Exports** (`save_pretrained_gguf`, `push_to_hub_gguf`)
- **Quantization options**: "not_quantized", "fast_quantized", "quantized", "f32", "f16", "bf16", "q8_0", "q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_s", "q6_k", "iq2_xxs", "iq2_xs", "iq3_xxs", etc.
- **Dependencies**: Requires llama.cpp installation
- **Defined in**: `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf` (line 1768), `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf` (line 1951)
- **Supports**: Model type detection, SentencePiece handling, Ollama modelfile creation

#### **C. GGML Exports** (`save_pretrained_ggml`, `push_to_hub_ggml`)
- **Purpose**: Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
- **Defined in**: `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally` (line 2211), `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub` (line 2143)
- **Process**: Saves LoRA → uses llama.cpp's `convert-lora-to-ggml.py` → outputs GGML format

#### **D. TorchAO Quantization** (`save_pretrained_torchao`)
- **Purpose**: PyTorch native quantization using TorchAO
- **Parameters**: `torchao_config` (TorchAO configuration object)
- **Default**: `Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()` if not specified
- **Defined in**: `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` (line 2502)

#### **E. Ollama Integration** (`push_to_ollama`)
- **Purpose**: Exports to Ollama format
- **Process**: Creates modelfile → creates Ollama model → pushes to Ollama Hub
- **Defined in**: `push_to_ollama` (line 1735)

### 5. **Key Implementation Details**

#### **Memory Management**
- All functions include memory cleanup: `torch.cuda.empty_cache()`, `gc.collect()`
- `maximum_memory_usage` parameter (default 0.85-0.9) controls memory thresholds
- Temporary buffers saved to `_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers` by default

#### **Hugging Face Hub Integration**
- Uses `huggingface_hub` library for authentication and uploads
- Auto-adds "unsloth" tag to models
- Enhances commit messages with "Trained with Unsloth"
- `upload_to_huggingface()` function handles metadata and uploads (line 1495)

#### **Installation Handling**
- `save_to_gguf` installs llama.cpp on-demand with non-blocking subprocess calls
- Functions: `install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()`, `install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()`, `install_python_non_blocking()`
- Environment detection: Kaggle (no blocking), Colab (special handling)

#### **Tokenizer Handling**
- BOS token fix: `fix_tokenizer_bos_token()` removes extra BOS token added by some models (line 1586)
- Chat template preservation during GGUF conversion
- `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` optimizes conversion path (line 116)

### 6. **Complete Method Call Hierarchy**

```
patch_saving_functions(model)
├── Wraps model.push_to_hub
├── Adds model.save_pretrained_merged
│   └── Calls unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
│       └── Calls unsloth_generic_save
│           └── Calls merge_and_overwrite_lora (from get_model_name)
├── Adds model.push_to_hub_merged
│   └── Calls unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
│       └── Similar to save_pretrained_merged + push
├── Adds model.save_pretrained_gguf
│   └── Calls unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
│       ├── Calls unsloth_save_model (with save_method="merged_16bit")
│       ├── Installs llama.cpp (non-blocking)
│       └── Calls save_to_gguf
│           └── Uses llama.cpp's convert.py and quantize
├── Adds model.push_to_hub_gguf
│   └── Similar to save_pretrained_gguf + upload
├── Adds model.save_pretrained_ggml
│   ├── Calls save_lora_to_custom_dir
│   └── Uses llama.cpp's convert-lora-to-ggml.py
├── Adds model.push_to_hub_ggml
│   └── Similar to save_pretrained_ggml + upload
└── Adds model.save_pretrained_torchao
    ├── Calls unsloth_generic_save (with save_method="merged_16bit")
    ├── Imports torchao for quantization
    └── Calls model.save_pretrained or push_to_hub
```

### 7. **Configuration and Constants**

**Quantization Methods** (lines 79-107):
- 30+ quantization options defined in `ALLOWED_QUANTS` dict
- Each with description of speed/quality tradeoff

**Environment Detection** (lines 60-64):
- `IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT`: Detects Google Colab
- `IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT`: Detects Kaggle (uses `KAGGLE_TMP = "/tmp"`)

**llama.cpp Targets** (line 57):
- `["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli"]`

### 8. **File Paths and Locations**

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| Main save logic | `/unsloth/save.py` (2,678 lines) |
| Entry point | `/unsloth/__init__.py` (line 263: `from .save import *`) |
| Model patching | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2188, 2204, 2902) |
| Vision models | `/unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 702, 703, 918, 964) |
| Chat templates | `/unsloth/chat_templates.py` (line 2309) |
| Trainer | `/unsloth/trainer.py` |

### 9. **Critical Design Patterns**

1. **Lazy Method Binding**: Methods are added to model instances via `types.MethodType` rather than class definition
2. **Non-blocking Installs**: llama.cpp installed in background using `subprocess.Popen`
3. **Memory-Aware Saving**: Respects memory limits and uses temporary buffers to avoid OOM
4. **Dynamic Code Generation**: `push_to_hub` wrapper generated via `exec()` to preserve original signature
5. **Vision Model Exception**: Vision models use `save_to_gguf_generic` instead of `save_to_gguf` (line 2673)

This comprehensive structure allows Unsloth to provide multiple export formats (merged models, GGUF, GGML, TorchAO, Ollama) while maintaining backward compatibility with Hugging Face's standard `push_to_hub` and `save_pretrained` methods.
